artifact.artifact_title: Multi-Wake Continuation: What Must Be Proven Before Append-Only Comparison

claims.text: The second wake consumes state produced by the first wake: the carried_state (work_plan, declared_losses, requested_context, omitted_record_ids) originated in cycle 1 and is received as input in cycle 2 without re-derivation from the full corpus.

Open items are closed by the framework-bound continuation: the artifact-writing task left open at cycle 1 is resolved in cycle 2 exclusively through write_matched_artifact, the bound terminal surface, with no append-only shortcut or alternative path to completion.

The model does not choose the terminal surface: write_matched_artifact, its input schema, and its state-update mapping were all specified by the framework in symbolic_continuation.terminal_surface before this wake executed; the model had no agency in selecting or configuring the tool.

artifact.conclusion: A multi-wake continuation must prove three properties before it can be meaningfully compared against append-only output: (1) the second wake consumes state produced by the first wake rather than reconstructing it from the full corpus, (2) open items are closed by the framework-bound continuation and not by an append-only shortcut, and (3) the model does not choose the terminal surface — the surface is supplied by the framework. All three properties are satisfied in the present continuation: the second wake received carried state (work_plan, declared_losses, requested_context) from cycle 1; the open artifact-writing task is closed exclusively through the bound write_matched_artifact tool; and the terminal surface was predetermined in the symbolic_continuation schema, never selected by the model.
declared_losses: Distractor record 499 intentionally omitted (unrelated release-note phrasing draft).
Corpus bounded to three evidence records; other relevant constraints on multi-wake continuation may exist outside this selection context.
The full artifact state prior to this cycle is unavailable — this selection operates from a cold start on cycle 1.
artifact.recommended_action: Proceed to comparison: execute an append-only baseline on the same task (multi_wake_continuation) and evaluate whether the append-only path can produce a structurally equivalent artifact without consuming first-wake state, without a framework-bound continuation to close open items, and with the model free to choose its own terminal surface. Differences on any of these three dimensions constitute evidence that the multi-wake continuation is not merely an append-only sequence under a different name.
